expectations.text: SOL-01: Solution JSON has solution_type: project
expectations.evidence: The solution.json has no 'solution_type' field at all. The top-level keys are: slug, proposition_slug, feature_slug, market_slug, product_slug, generated, title, summary, implementation_plan, pricing, compliance_coverage, buying_committee_alignment.
implementation_plan.phases contains 4 phases: P1 Assessment & Discovery, P2 Mobilize & Landing Zone, P3 Migration Waves, P4 Optimize & Transition.

expectations.text: SOL-03: All four pricing tiers present (proof_of_value, small, medium, large)
expectations.evidence: No pricing tiers exist. The pricing section contains a single flat rate card and effort summary totaling EUR 2.67M. There are no proof_of_value, small, medium, or large tier structures.
No 'cost_model' field exists in the solution JSON. Pricing data is under a 'pricing' key with effort_summary broken by phase (not by tier). No 'effort_by_tier' structure is present.
target_margin_pct: 30, margin_amount: 800571 (EUR). Total delivery cost 1,868,000 vs total price 2,670,000. Margin is positive.

expectations.text: SOL-06: Review assessment JSON produced with all three perspectives
expectations.evidence: review-assessment.json contains three perspectives: 'Procurement Decision-Maker', 'Provider-Side Solution Architect', and 'Client-Side Solution Architect'.

expectations.text: SOL-07: Each perspective has 5 scored criteria
expectations.evidence: Procurement: 5 criteria (3 pass, 2 warn). Provider SA: 5 criteria (4 pass, 1 warn). Client SA: 5 criteria (3 pass, 2 warn). All 15 criteria have score values.
The verdict section has overall_score: 'pass_with_conditions', which is not one of the required values (accept, revise, reject). The expected enum was not followed.

expectations.text: SOL-09: If verdict was revise, a second assessment exists with improved scores
expectations.evidence: The verdict was 'pass_with_conditions' (not 'revise'), so this conditional assertion is satisfied vacuously. No second assessment was required.

expectations.text: SOL-10: Phase descriptions are in German (portfolio language: de)
expectations.evidence: All four phase descriptions are in German. Example from P1: 'Vollstaendige Inventarisierung der Workload-Landschaft, Abhaengigkeitsanalyse, KRITIS-Relevanz-Klassifizierung und 6R-Strategiezuordnung...'

claims.claim: Solution addresses KRITIS-Compliance, OT/IT convergence, and skills shortage
claims.evidence: BSI-C5, NIS2, KRITIS-DachG are covered in compliance_coverage. OT/IT segmentation addressed in P2. Fachkräftemangel mentioned in P3 risks and buying committee alignment.
Delivery cost 1,868,000 + margin 800,571 = 2,668,571 rounded to 2,670,000. Margin percentage: 800,571/2,668,571 = 30.0%.
All 15 criteria scored either 'pass' (11) or 'warn' (4). fail_count: 0 in verdict.
P1: 6 weeks + P2: 8 weeks + P3: 24 weeks + P4: 6 weeks = 44 weeks. Matches total_duration_weeks: 44.

suggestions.assertion: SOL-09: If verdict was revise, a second assessment exists with improved scores
suggestions.reason: This assertion is vacuously true whenever the verdict is not 'revise'. Combined with SOL-08 failing (verdict used wrong enum), this means the revise-and-improve loop is never actually tested. Consider making SOL-08 a prerequisite for SOL-09, or auto-failing SOL-09 when SOL-08 fails.

This only checks a single global margin. The assertion was designed for tiered pricing (proof_of_value, small, medium, large) where each tier should have a positive margin. With flat pricing, a single positive number satisfies it trivially. Consider coupling this with SOL-03 so margin validation is per-tier.

No assertion checks whether the solution references the correct proposition inputs (market pain points, product capabilities). The baseline produced high-quality German content that happens to match the domain, but there is no structural check that it actually consumed the proposition data rather than hallucinating plausible content.

eval_feedback.overall: The baseline (no skill) produced a well-structured solution and review but missed key schema requirements: no solution_type field, no pricing tiers, no cost_model/effort_by_tier, and used a non-standard verdict enum. These are exactly the structural gaps a skill should enforce.
